Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: footwear by function [300252213], footwear [300209280], accessories by location on the legs or feet [300211599], worn costume accessories [300209274], costume accessories [300209273], costume (mode of fashion) [300178802], Costume (hierarchy name) [300209261], Furnishings and Equipment (hierarchy name) [300264551], Objects Facet [300264092]

Non-Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: wooden shoes [300438700], shoes by construction [300252215], shoes (footwear) [300046065], footwear by form [300252212], footwear [300209280], accessories by location on the legs or feet [300211599], worn costume accessories [300209274], costume accessories [300209273], costume (mode of fashion) [300178802], Costume (hierarchy name) [300209261], Furnishings and Equipment (hierarchy name) [300264551], Objects Facet [300264092]

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: Footwear made partly or wholly of wood, with a thick sole usually of wood, but sometimes of leather or cork; originally to protect the foot, hosiery, or other footwear. The term "clogs" may also apply to shoes in the style of wooden clogs, but made of other materials. For overshoes consisting of a sole set on bands of iron, use "pattens."

Schoeisel dat gedeeltelijk of geheel van hout is gemaakt met een dikke zool die meestal van hout is maar soms ook van leer of kurk, ter bescherming van de voet, van kousen of sokken of van ander schoeisel. Gebruik `steltschoenen' vooroverschoenen die bestaan uit een zool die is geplaatst op ijzeren banden.
Calzature in parte o interamente in legno, con una suola spessa in legno, ma a volte in pelle o sughero, per proteggere il piede, la calzetteria, o altre calzature.

Fußbekleidung, die zu Teilen oder im Ganzen aus Holz hergestellt wurde, mit einer dicken Sohle gewöhnlich aus Holz, aber manchmal auch aus Leder oder Kork, um die Füße, Strumpfwaren oder andere Fußbekleidung zu schützen. Für Überschuhe, die aus einer Sohlengarnitur aus Eisenbändern bestehen, verwende "Patte".

Source.Source_ID: 2000001681/Livandi, Thesaurus of Clothing and Textile Terms, Personal Artifacts (1990)
2000030201/National Museum of American History, Dictionary of Costume (n.d.)
2000030231/Thornton and Swann, Glossary of Shoe Terms (1986)

Term_Source.Page: A wooden or wooden-soled shoe, boot or overshoe. 17th-18thc clog overshoes had only a wedge of wood.

Source.Source_ID: 2000046682/Random House Dictionary of the English Language (1987)
